title: Valerianella dasycarpa
taxonomy: Valerianella dasycarpa is a species of plant in the family Valerianaceae. It belongs to the genus Valerianella, which includes other small, annual or biennial plants. Valerianella dasycarpa is a member of the order Dipsacales and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Valerianaceae is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as valerian or centranthus. This classification places Valerianella dasycarpa within the broader angiosperm lineage.

intro: Valerianella dasycarpa, also known as the hairy corn salad or hairy valerianella, is a perennial plant species in the family Valerianaceae, characterized by its edible leaves and stems. The plant is rich in health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Valerianella dasycarpa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their potential to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
